text: So: the honest target user today is a **solo developer running multiple coding-agent hosts (Claude Code + Codex + …) who wants durable identity and shared awareness across sessions/devices** — but the docs themselves warn against over-indexing on the "agents collide on the same file" framing as the pitch, since that's unproven (see Bets Q1) and explicitly demoted to "ceiling, not floor" in value-layers.md. [...] WHO THEY ARE: Solo/small-team dev running 2+ coding agents (Claude Code + Codex, or +Cursor) on one repo simultaneously. Self-identifies as an "agent power user" — early adopter, treats parallel agents as a status/leverage signal (see "agent fleet," "swarm," "20-30 sessions in parallel" bragging language on dev blogs/X). Values: raw velocity, being ahead of the curve, engineering taste (they'll judge your code/API design, not just your prose). Brags about: commit throughput, number of parallel agents, being an early adopter of frontier workflow patterns. Fears: (1) unsupervised agents making bad silent decisions overnight — HN consensus is explicitly "don't trust AI agents unsupervised," verification capacity is called the real bottleneck; (2) losing hours to untangling merge chaos that erases the productivity win; (3) looking naive/hyped-up in front of peers — this audience punishes marketing-speak hard.
